name: Ticrynafen
description: Ticrynafen, or tienilic acid, is a diuretic drug with uric acid-lowering (uricosuric) action, formerly marketed for the treatment of hypertension. It was withdrawn in 1982, shortly after its introduction to the market, after case reports in the United States indicated a link between the use of ticrynafen and hepatitis. (Manier et al., 1982)

interactions.name: Sarilumab
interactions.description: Sarilumab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Ticrynafen.

interactions.name: Sodium phosphate
interactions.description: Ticrynafen may increase the nephrotoxic activities of Sodium phosphate.

interactions.name: Candesartan cilexetil
interactions.description: The risk or severity of hypotension can be increased when Candesartan cilexetil is combined with Ticrynafen.

interactions.name: Acemetacin
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Ticrynafen can be decreased when used in combination with Acemetacin.

interactions.name: Amifostine
interactions.description: Ticrynafen may increase the hypotensive activities of Amifostine.
